content.full_text:
November 16, 1978

Theresa L. Claassen  
El Macero Republican Women's Club  
Box 2527  
El Macero, California 95618

Dear Ms. Claassen:

Thank you for your letter of November 8, 1978, in which you ask a number of questions regarding campaign contributions.

A club may make up to $499 in campaign contributions in a calendar year without being required to register as a committee. Once the club makes contributions of $500 or more, it must file a Statement of Organization and campaign statements.

Although we do not regulate the tax laws, it is my understanding that a couple can claim a federal tax credit of 50% up to $50. Thus, if a couple makes a contribution of $100, it can reduce its federal taxes by $50. The state law does not permit tax credits, only deductions, which are only helpful for a couple that itemizes deductions. Under state law, a couple may take up to $200 in deductions. Thus, in the highest tax bracket of 11 percent, the couple would only reduce their taxes by $22 for a $200 contribution. Please check with the Franchise Tax Board or Internal Revenue Service for further information on the tax laws.

I would be happy to discuss with you any Political Reform Act requirements.

Sincerely,

Robert M. Stern  
General Counsel

El Macero Republican Women's Club  
BOX 2527 • EL MACERO, CALIFORNIA 95618

November 8, 1978

Mrs. Bob Stern  
Attorney for Fair Political Practices  
Sacramento Calif.

Dear sir:

For our information and records we need to have a statement regarding the total maximum contributions a club can make toward all candidates in one year without the necessity of filing forms.

It is our understanding that up to $499-$500 no filing is required.

Also, is it $100 that a couple can deduct ($50 each) on income tax returns?

A prompt reply will be appreciated.

Very truly yours,

Theresa L. Claassen
